title: Archived Technical Consultation on Health Canada's Proposal to Amend the Food and Drug Regulations to Permit the use of cross-linked carboxymethyl cellulose in table-top sweetener tablets - Canada.ca
content:
## Archived Technical Consultation on Health Canada's Proposal to Amend the Food and Drug Regulations to Permit the use of cross-linked carboxymethyl cellulose in table-top sweetener tablets
Notice to the reader: The online consultation is now closed.
Health Canada's Food Directorate has recently completed a detailed safety assessment of 'cross-linked carboxymethyl celluose', for use as a food additive in tablet formulations of sucralose-containing table-top sweeteners.
Since no safety concerns have been raised through this assessment, the department is proposing amendments to Division 16, Part B of the Regulations to enable the use described in the consultation document .  The purpose of this communication is to seek comments on this proposed action.
This consultation is open for comment starting July 28, 2011 until October 11, 2011 (75 calendar days) . Comments on this proposal may be submitted in writing, by regular mail or electronically. If you are submitting your comments electronically, please use the words " Cross-Linked CMC " in the subject box of your e-mail.
sche-ann@hc-sc.gc.ca
Bureau of Chemical Safety Chemical Health Hazard Assessment Division 251 Sir Frederick Banting Drive Health Canada, Tunney's Pasture Address Locator: 2201C Ottawa, Ontario K1A 0L2
Phone: 613-957-0973 Fax: 613-954-4674

## Consultation Document
Information Document on Health Canada's Proposal to amend the Food and Drug Regulations to allow the use of cross-linked carboxymethyl cellulose in table-top sweetener tablets
Submissions must be received by 11:59 p.m. EST, October 11, 2011.

## Supporting Information

## Reporting to Canadians
Health Canada will make the results of this consultation available on this Web site. If you have any questions, contact us at sche-ann@hc-sc.gc.ca
